verdict_reasoning: HSPA5/GRP78/BiP is canonically an ER-luminal HSP70 chaperone, but has extensive documented cell-surface display on tumor cells, stressed cells, and during ER stress / UPR activation (csGRP78). The cell-surface pool is a well-established cancer target with multiple clinical-stage antibody, peptide, and CAR-T programs directed at csGRP78 on intact tumor cells. This is classic cell-state-induced ecto-presentation of a nominally intracellular chaperone — not pMHC, not secreted equilibrium — the protein body itself is externalized on the outer leaflet under stress / oncogenic states.
